subject: subtomogram reconstruction crashes - network issue?
body:
Hi there, 

I am processing a 110 tomogram dataset, and everything went ok until 
subtomogram export. However, during subtomogram reconstruction (as 
Volumes), warp always crashes at some point. I am using a 180 px box with 5 
Å/px. 
I have now manually deselected most tomograms, and only export 4 or 8 
tomograms at a time, which seems to work. I have also unselected the "Use 
CPU" option. Looking at the windows task manager Network utilisation 
monitor, I noticed that it sometimes briefly reaches 100%  when all 4 GPUs 
are active, so I am suspecting that the crashes occur when network capacity 
is reached or exceeded. So I think I have two options

   1. Reduce the number of GPUs used for processing to reduce I/O
   2. Increase bandwidth.

Regarding 2. our machine lists 3 ethernet cards, only one of which is used. 
Would plugging in two additional ethernet cables incrase the i/o capacity 
of our machine, and would Warp be able to utilize these?

Thanks a lot in advance,
Matthias
